name: Google Gemini / ADK — context caching and window compression
context_primitives: **Vertex/Gemini context caching**: implicit caching (Gemini 2.5+ discounts on repeated prefixes) and explicit cache API for large static context (docs, multimodal). **ADK** exposes ContextCacheConfig (TTL, min tokens) and **context_window_compression** in RunConfig for long-running agents—platform focuses on **cost/latency of repeated prefixes** more than application semantic memory. Million-token windows reduce pressure but do not replace curation.
deployment_pattern: Enable caching for static system+docs blocks; ADK compression for marathon sessions; still design tool-output clearing in agent loop.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Google publishes caching economics; agent memory quality benchmarks cited by Mem0/Zep not native to ADK docs.
ecosystem_integrations: Vertex AI, Gemini API, ADK multi-agent apps.
differentiators: vs. **Alex**: Google leads with **cache hit economics** on huge windows; Alex leads with **semantic selection** (what spans matter) on observability payloads. Complementary: cache stable instructions, still truncate tool spam like Alex.
significance_for_builders: If your workload repeats massive static context (specs, traces templates), **cache** first; if dynamic trace search dominates, **sub-agents + retrieval** (Alex) beat caching alone.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Gemini 2.5 implicit caching blog May 2025; ADK context caching docs.

primary_sources.title: Google Developers Blog — Gemini 2.5 implicit caching
